name: Monasterio de Santa María de Valdediós from Villaviciosa
name_original: Monasterio de Santa María de Valdediós desde Villaviciosa
region_intro: Road cycling around Villaviciosa, nestled in Asturias, offers diverse landscapes for cyclists. The region features a blend of coastal beauty, lush green valleys, and rolling hills, providing varied experiences. Its terrain ranges from sea level to 300 meters, encompassing well-preserved beaches, rural environments, and challenging ascents. This area is known for its apple orchards and the Villaviciosa Estuary, a Partial Nature Reserve. Best road cycling routes around Villaviciosa The most popular road cycling route is Les Praeres and Alto de La Campa Loop, a 38.7 miles (62.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 41 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gains through mountainous paths. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Monasterio de Santa María de Valdediós from Villaviciosa, an easy 11.3 miles (18.2 km) path. This route leads to cultural landmarks like the pre-Romanesque Church of San Salvador. Local road cyclists also love the Town Hall Square – La Providencia Viewpoint loop from Villaviciosa, a 38.9 miles (62.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with notable viewpoints, often completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes. Road cycling around Villaviciosa is defined by coastal routes, green valleys, and rolling hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Villaviciosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1,200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villaviciosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villaviciosa?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes around Villaviciosa, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Villaviciosa?
A: Villaviciosa offers diverse terrain, from coastal routes with well-preserved beaches and the Villaviciosa Estuary to lush green valleys, rolling hills, and challenging mountainous paths. The municipality's altitude varies from sea level to 300 meters, providing varied experiences.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Villaviciosa?
A: Yes, Villaviciosa has several easy road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077444018/monasterio-de-santa-maria-de-valdedios-from-villaviciosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Santa María de Valdediós from Villaviciosa&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 11.3-mile (18.2 km) path that leads to cultural landmarks.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in Villaviciosa?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Villaviciosa offers routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070246229/les-praeres-and-alto-de-la-campa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Praeres and Alto de La Campa Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 38.7-mile (62.2 km) route featuring substantial climbs. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20536347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall Square – La Providencia Viewpoint loop from Villaviciosa&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 38.9 miles (62.6 km) with notable elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Villaviciosa area?
A: Yes, many routes in Villaviciosa are designed as loops. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070246229/les-praeres-and-alto-de-la-campa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Praeres and Alto de La Campa Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Campa – Gargantadá loop from Church of San Salvador de Valdediós&lt;/a&gt;. These allow you to start and finish at the same point.

Q: What cultural landmarks can I explore by road bike in Villaviciosa?
A: You can cycle to significant cultural sites such as the pre-Romanesque Church of San Salvador de Valdediós and the Cistercian Monastery of Santa María la Real. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077444018/monasterio-de-santa-maria-de-valdedios-from-villaviciosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Santa María de Valdediós from Villaviciosa&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to visit these historical points.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Villaviciosa?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer stunning vistas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20536347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall Square – La Providencia Viewpoint loop from Villaviciosa&lt;/a&gt; includes notable viewpoints. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1036457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Sol Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5616840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Gijón and the Coast from Pico del Sol&lt;/a&gt; in the surrounding peaks area.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by natural features like waterfalls or the estuary?
A: Yes, Villaviciosa's natural beauty is a highlight. You can cycle through areas near the Villaviciosa Estuary, a Partial Nature Reserve known for its ecological value. While specific road cycling routes directly to waterfalls aren't listed, the region features several beautiful waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1821428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molín de la Peña and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4925754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mexona Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored in conjunction with your rides.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villaviciosa?
A: Villaviciosa offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, especially when the apple orchards ('pumaradas') are in bloom or harvest. Summer is also popular, though coastal routes might offer a cooler breeze.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy about the routes in Villaviciosa?
A: The komoot community highly rates the road cycling routes in Villaviciosa, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from coastal views to lush valleys and challenging climbs, as well as the opportunity to explore cultural landmarks and natural reserves like the Villaviciosa Estuary.

Q: Are there routes that allow me to experience both coastal and rural charm?
A: Yes, Villaviciosa is uniquely positioned to offer both. Many routes connect the coastal areas, including Rodiles Beach and the fishing village of Tazones, with the rural interior characterized by apple orchards and charming villages. This blend provides a rich and varied cycling experience.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty road cycling routes in Villaviciosa?
A: Yes, there are numerous moderate routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39562578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ría de Villaviciosa – Dársena del Puntal loop from Piñera&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 50.6-kilometer route that takes you along the estuary, offering a balanced challenge and scenic views.
tips: Road without much traffic that brings us closer to the Monastery from the town of Villabona. An ideal stretch for road bike lovers.

The Monastery of Santa María de Valdediós is in a good state of conservation and is ideal for those seeking a moment of peace and tranquility. You can pay the entrance fee and follow some audio guides inside the architectural complex.
